description: Controls how users enter rooms via invitation, direct join, or knock. Enforces join rules and rate-limits invitations. Supports third-party invitations via identity servers.

actors.name: Knocking User
actors.description: User requesting permission to join a knock-enabled room

actors.name: Homeserver
actors.description: Server enforcing join rules and rate limits

fields.name: room_id
fields.label: Room being joined or invited to

fields.name: membership
fields.label: Current membership state of the user in the room

fields.name: join_rule
fields.label: Rule governing how users may join the room

fields.name: allow_conditions
fields.label: For restricted rooms, the set of conditions that permit joining

fields.name: third_party_medium
fields.label: Medium of a third-party identifier (email, phone)

fields.name: transaction_id
fields.label: Client-provided identifier for idempotent invite requests

values.description: User has no active membership in the room
User has been invited and may accept or decline
User is a full member of the room
User has requested access and is awaiting approval
User has been prohibited from joining

transitions.description: Inviter with sufficient power level sends an invite
User joins a public or restricted room without invitation
User knocks on a knock-enabled room
Room member approves the knock by sending an invite

invitations: Invitations require the inviter to have a power level meeting the room's invite threshold
Invitation sending is rate-limited per room, per recipient, and per inviter
Shadow-banned users cannot send invitations; their requests appear to succeed but are not delivered
Duplicate invitations (same sender, recipient, and content) are deduplicated
Banned users may not be invited; they must be unbanned first
joining: Public rooms allow any authenticated user to join without invitation
Restricted rooms require the joining user to meet at least one of the declared allow conditions
Knock-enabled rooms require a moderator to approve the knock before the knocker can join
Third-party invites are validated through an identity server before delivery
Users cannot knock if they already have an active invitation or membership

given: inviter's power level meets the room's invite threshold
invitee is not already a member or banned
invite_sent.result: Invitee receives the invite and may accept or decline

join_public_room.result: User is immediately a full member

given: user meets at least one allow condition
join_restricted_room.result: User joins after satisfying the restriction conditions

given: user is not already a member, banned, or has a pending invite
knock_sent.result: Room members are notified and may approve the knock

given: rate limit threshold exceeded for room, invitee, or inviter
invite_rate_limited.result: Invite rejected until rate limit window resets

given: inviter's power level is below the room's invite threshold
user is banned from the room
join_denied_banned.result: Join rejected; user must be unbanned by a moderator first

given: room join rule does not permit knocking
knock_denied_rule.result: Knock rejected; room does not support knock workflow

errors.message: You have sent too many invitations recently. Please wait before sending more.
You do not have permission to invite users to this room
You have been banned from this room
You do not meet the requirements to join this room
This room does not accept knock requests
You cannot knock on a room you are already in

events.name: membership.invited
events.description: A user has been invited to a room

events.name: membership.joined
events.description: A user has become a full member of a room

events.name: membership.knocked
events.description: A user has requested access to a knock-enabled room

events.name: membership.left
events.description: A user has left or been removed from a room

related.reason: Invite, kick, and ban thresholds are defined in the room power levels
Join rules are established in the room's initial state at creation
Third-party invitations are validated through an identity server
Cross-server invitations are delivered and co-signed via federation

goals.description: Controls how users enter rooms via invitation, direct join, or knock. Enforces join rules and rate-limits invitations. Supports third-party invitations via identity servers.

constraints.description: Sensitive fields must be encrypted at rest and never logged in plaintext

human_checkpoints: before transitioning to a terminal state

tradeoffs.reason: access control must enforce least-privilege principle

invariants: sensitive fields are never logged in plaintext
all data access is authenticated and authorized
error messages never expose internal system details
state transitions follow the defined state machine — no illegal transitions
